brief_title: Prostate Cancer Education in African American Men
official_title: Prostate Cancer Education in African American Men
brief_summary: This study evaluates the efficacy of a tailored telephone intervention to promote informed decision making about prostate cancer testing among predominantly immigrant black men.
detailed_description: African American and African-Caribbean men have the greatest prostate cancer incidence and mortality rate. Professional organizations provide conflicting recommendations regarding prostate cancer testing but generally agree that men learn about the risks and benefits of testing and share in decisions about testing based on their personal preferences. This study was designed to assess the effects of a decision support intervention on men's knowledge about prostate cancer testing, participation in medical decisions about testing, decision conflict related to testing, and the congruence between prostate cancer testing intentions and behaviors. A randomized controlled trial was conducted. Participants were randomized into one of two conditions: (a) tailored telephone education about prostate cancer testing (intervention group) or (b) tailored telephone education about national guidelines for fruit and vegetable consumption (attention control group).

interventions.name: Prostate Cancer Screening Education
interventions.description: tailored telephone education on prostate cancer testing

interventions.name: Fruit and Vegetable Intake Education
interventions.description: tailored telephone education about fruit and vegetable consumption

eligibility.criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* 45 to 70 year old
* African descent
* accessible by telephone
* have primary care physician

Exclusion Criteria:

* Prostate cancer test in 12 months prior to enrollment
* History of prostate cancer

primary_outcomes.measure: congruence between prostate cancer testing intention and behavior
primary_outcomes.description: Congruence between men's stated intentions to get tested and their actual testing behavior, validated by medical claims at 1 and 2 year follow-up. Intention-behavior agreement was coded as congruent (1), whereas disagreement was coded as incongruent (0).

primary_outcomes.measure: Knowledge about prostate cancer and prostate cancer tests
primary_outcomes.description: 12 item knowledge index with questions on testing, risk factors and epidemiology, and treatment effectiveness and side effects. Percent correct was used as the outcome measure.
Used a modified version of the 16 item Decisional Conflict Scale with the 3 level response category suggested for low literacy populations.

primary_outcomes.measure: Verified doctor visit to discuss prostate cancer testing
primary_outcomes.description: Visit with physician to talk about prostate cancer testing, with visit verified through medical claims records
